verdict: PASS — all checks clear; 1490 words within [1200,1600]; primary resource cited; six sections in order; no frontmatter; no forward references; no scope_creep; faithfulness to corpus confirmed across 10 sampled assertions; beginner readability and formatting standards met.

format.notes: First non-empty line is '# Probability basics — likelihood, events, outcomes'. Sidecar parses with word_count, estimated_minutes, depth_profile, citations. Sidecar lacks a 'sections' array field but all substantive fields present — not treated as hard fail. Sections in order: Overview / Key Concepts / Worked Example / In Practice / Key Takeaways / References.

length.notes: 1490 / 200 wpm = 7.45 min. Both word count and minute estimate within target ranges.

citations.notes: Primary resource [1] cited 7 times. All inline markers [1][2][3] have bibliography entries. No orphans.

no_forward_references.notes: All topic back-references are to permitted prior topics (1.3, 1.4, 3.4). 'probability distributions' in In Practice (line 151) is a bare mention-and-defer with topic pointer to 7.4 — no defining clause, not bolded. Compliant.

faithfulness.notes: 10 assertions sampled across all sections. All trace to corpus sections 5.1–5.8 and 7. No fabricated claims detected.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined on first use with bold-term/plain-meaning shape (Probability, Outcome, Sample space, Event, Complement, LLM, token). No paragraphs introduce 3+ new terms at once. Concrete examples (coin, die, cards) precede abstractions throughout. No hedging phrases found. Sentence length target met; learner addressed as 'you' throughout.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 4 sentences. Worked Example section presents the seven-step counting method as a numbered list. Tables used for comparisons (probability scale, sample space, events, complement, Do/Don't). Bold-term definitions used for all key vocabulary. Inline examples appear alongside concept introductions in Key Concepts, not only deferred to Worked Example.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights appear in the reading.
